1: So they brought the ark of God, and set it in the midst of the tent that David had pitched for it: and they offered burnt sacrifices and peace offerings before God.
2: And when David had made an end of offering the burnt offerings and the peace offerings, he blessed the people in the name of the LORD LORD .
3: And he dealt to every one of Israel, both man and woman, to every one a loaf of bread, and a good piece of flesh, and a flagon of wine of wine .
4: And he appointed certain certain of the Levites to minister before the ark of the LORD LORD , and to record, and to thank and praise the LORD LORD God of Israel:
5: Asaph the chief, and next to him Zechariah, Jeiel, and Shemiramoth, and Jehiel, and Mattithiah, and Eliab, and Benaiah, and Obed-edom: and Jeiel with psalteries and with harps; but Asaph made a sound with cymbals;
6: Benaiah also and Jahaziel the priests with trumpets continually before the ark of the covenant of God.
7: Then on that day David delivered first this psalm this psalm to thank the LORD LORD into the hand of Asaph and his brethren.
8: Make known his deeds among the people.
9: Talk ye of all his wondrous works.
10: Let the heart of them rejoice that seek the LORD LORD .
12: His wonders, and the judgments of his mouth;
13: Ye children of Jacob, his chosen ones.
14: His judgments are are in all the earth.
15: The word which which he commanded to a thousand generations;
16: And of his oath unto Isaac;
17: And And to Israel for for an everlasting covenant,
19: Even a few, and strangers in it.
20: And from one one kingdom to another people;
21: Yea, he reproved kings for their sakes,
22: And do my prophets no harm.
23: Shew forth from day to day his salvation.
24: His marvellous works among all nations.
25: He also is is to be feared above all gods.
26: But the LORD LORD made the heavens.
27: Strength and gladness are are in his place.
28: Give unto the LORD LORD glory and strength.
29: Worship the LORD LORD in the beauty of holiness.
30: The world also shall be stable, that it be not moved.
31: And let men men say among the nations, The LORD LORD reigneth.
32: Let the fields rejoice, and all that is is therein.
33: Because he cometh to judge the earth.
34: For his mercy endureth endureth for ever.
35: And And glory in thy praise.
36: And all the people said, Amen, and praised the LORD LORD .
37: So he left there before the ark of the covenant of the LORD LORD Asaph and his brethren, to minister before the ark continually, as every day's work required:
38: and Obed-edom with their brethren, threescore and eight; Obed-edom also the son of Jeduthun and Hosah to be to be porters:
39: and Zadok the priest, and his brethren the priests, before the tabernacle of the LORD LORD in the high place that was was at Gibeon,
40: to offer burnt offerings unto the LORD LORD upon the altar of the burnt offering continually morning and evening, and to do to do according to all that is written in the law of the LORD LORD , which he commanded Israel;
41: and with them Heman and Jeduthun, and the rest that were chosen, who were expressed by name, to give thanks to the LORD LORD , because his mercy endureth endureth for ever;
42: and with them Heman and Jeduthun with trumpets and cymbals for those that should make a sound, and with musical instruments of God. And the sons of Jeduthun were were porters.
43: And all the people departed every man to his house: and David returned to bless his house.
